description:
Goal: remove the temporary clause-head matching scaffolding from Compiler2 bodies once entry dispatch exists.

Change:
- Move function-entry matching responsibility fully into `EntryDispatch(function)` and stop treating `LoweredBody` as the source of truth for clause-head setup/guard selection.
- Shrink or reshape the per-clause body representation so it carries clause bodies and body-local structure, not a second entry matcher.
- Update any semantic consumers to read entry dispatch plus lowered bodies instead of clause-local rematching fields.

Why:
- Keeping entry dispatch and clause-local rematching in parallel leaves two sources of truth and makes later retraction/convergence work noisier than it needs to be.
- This is explicit scaffolding-removal work for the temporary body shape introduced before shared dispatch planning landed.

Checks:
- Compiler2 semantic and lowering tests continue to pass while entry dispatch remains the only entry-matching authority.
- No semantic consumer depends on clause-local setup/guard data for function-head selection.

Non-goals:
- No new body-site dispatch support.
- No new artifact work.
title: Compiler2 cleanup: remove clause-head matching scaffolding from lowered bodies
